text.title: Vendor Evaluation Scorecard Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Vendor Evaluation Scorecard application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Scorecard is shared (Sheet / Notion). All evaluators score independently before discussing. Reduces anchoring bias.
rule.rationale: Group discussion before independent scores anchors first speaker; independent first is the bias mitigation.

rule.statement: Weights set BEFORE scoring. After-the-fact weight adjustment = rationalization.
rule.rationale: Picking winner first + then weighting is the most common evaluation antipattern.

rule.statement: Real trial (≥2 weeks) on each top-2 candidate. Demo-only decisions repeatedly fail.
rule.rationale: Demos exclude failure modes; trial surfaces them in low-risk window.

rule.statement: Final decision recorded as ADR-equivalent: candidates, scores, weights, trial outcome, chosen + why.
rule.rationale: Without record, future evaluations repeat work + lose institutional memory.

rule.statement: Revisit decision at contract renewal date. Auto-renewal without re-eval = vendor lock-in compounds.
rule.rationale: Contract renewals are the natural evaluation triggers; missing them locks in suboptimal.
